violations.claim: Prove: ref is non-empty — code: const ref = args.find(...) || "HEAD~1"
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 267
; Prove: ref is non-empty — code: const ref = args.find(...) || "HEAD~1"
; find() returns undefined (no match) or a string; || "HEAD~1" covers all falsy cases

(declare-const find_returned Int)    ; 0=undefined, 1=empty_string, 2=nonempty_string
(declare-const ref_nonempty Int)     ; 1 = ref has at least one character

; Transition: ref = find_result || "HEAD~1"
; find returns undefined → ref = "HEAD~1" (non-empty)
(assert (=> (= find_returned 0) (= ref_nonempty 1)))
; find returns "" (falsy) → ref = "HEAD~1" (non-empty)
(assert (=> (= find_returned 1) (= ref_nonempty 1)))
; find returns non-empty string (truthy) → ref = that string (non-empty)
(assert (=> (= find_returned 2) (= ref_nonempty 1)))

; Attempt to falsify: ref is empty at line 267
(assert (= ref_nonempty 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: args is user-supplied; ref = first non-flag arg with no format check
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 267
; args is user-supplied; ref = first non-flag arg with no format check
; diffAgainst(ref) requires a valid git ref as precondition
; runDiff does not establish that precondition

(declare-const user_provided_positional Int)  ; 1 = user gave a positional arg
(declare-const user_ref_valid Int)            ; 1 = user's string is a valid git ref
(declare-const ref_valid Int)                 ; 1 = ref reaching diffAgainst is valid

; Transition: no positional arg → default "HEAD~1" (valid)
(assert (=> (= user_provided_positional 0) (= ref_valid 1)))
; Transition: positional arg present → ref = that arg, validity unconstrained by code
(assert (=> (= user_provided_positional 1) (= ref_valid user_ref_valid)))

; Violation: user passes an unrecognised string (e.g. "not@@a//ref")
(assert (= user_provided_positional 1))
(assert (= user_ref_valid 0))
(assert (= ref_valid 0))
(check-sat)
